pi_items.source: Verified against task description: 'Create a new process named Soybean Crushing (Custom)'
Verified against task description: 'Define two outputs: Soybean Oil (Custom) (180 kg)'
Verified against task description: 'Define two outputs: ... Soybean Meal (Custom) (800 kg)'

Verified against real market data: Soybean oil prices in 2024-2025 ranged from $0.69-$1.40/kg. The claimed $1.00/kg falls within this range and matches historical farmgate prices. Source: https://www.procurementresource.com/resource-center/soybean-oil-price-trends

Verified against real market data: Soybean meal prices in 2024-2025 ranged from $0.39-$0.82/kg. The claimed $0.40/kg is at the lower end but realistic and matches Q1 2025 Spain market data. Source: https://www.procurementresource.com/resource-center/soybean-meal-price-trends

Verified against soybean crushing yields: Standard crushing yields are ~18-19.8% oil. 180 kg from 1000 kg input = 18% yield. Source: https://www.ocl-journal.org/articles/ocl/full_html/2020/01/ocl200047s/ocl200047s.html

Verified against soybean crushing yields: Standard crushing yields are ~78-80% meal. 800 kg from 1000 kg input = 80% yield. Source: https://www.ocl-journal.org/articles/ocl/full_html/2020/01/ocl200047s/ocl200047s.html
Verified by economic allocation calculation: (180 kg × $1.00/kg) / ((180×1.00) + (800×0.40)) = 180/500 = 0.36. Code verification: Python calculation confirms exact match.
Verified by economic allocation calculation: (800 kg × $0.40/kg) / ((180×1.00) + (800×0.40)) = 320/500 = 0.64. Code verification: Python calculation confirms exact match.
Verified as reasonable tolerance: 0.02 represents ~5.6% for oil factor and ~3.1% for meal factor, appropriate for UI input verification and rounding errors.

privileged_info_summary: The soybean crushing allocation task uses realistic market prices: soybean oil at $1.00/kg (within 2024-2025 market range of $0.69-$1.40/kg) and soybean meal at $0.40/kg (at lower end of 2024-2025 range of $0.39-$0.82/kg). Production volumes reflect standard soybean crushing yields: 180 kg oil (18% yield) and 800 kg meal (80% yield) from 1000 kg input soybeans. Economic allocation factors are mathematically correct: 0.36 for oil and 0.64 for meal, calculated as the proportion of total product value. Mass balance accounts for ~2% processing loss (hulls/waste), realistic for actual crushing operations. All numerical values are verified against real-world data and established industry standards.
